info.name: Novellia-takehome API
info.description: A FHIR-backed REST API for patient health records. Serves data from an in-memory store loaded from a FHIR JSONL data file. No authentication required.

variable.description: Patient ID (e.g. noah-wyle, tracy-ifeachor, patrick-ball, katherine-lanasa, supriya-ganesh, fiona-dourif, taylor-dearden, isa-briones, gerran-howell, shabana-azeez, sepideh-moafi, ayesha-harris, shawn-hatosy, jalen-thomas-brooks, kristin-villanueva, brandon-mendez-homer). Case-insensitive.
The condition resource ID.

request.description: Returns all observations (vitals, labs) for the given patient. Duplicate observations (same code + datetime) are deduplicated, with 'amended' status taking precedence.

response.name: A list of observations for the patient.

request.description: Returns the raw binary content of the specified document for the given patient. The response Content-Type header reflects the document's original MIME type (e.g. text/plain, application/pdf).

response.name: Raw binary content of the document.

request.description: Returns a list of document summaries for the given patient, sorted by date descending. Each entry includes the document ID, status, date, authors, content type, and a reference to the backing binary resource. Use GET /patients/{id}/documents/{doc_id} to retrieve the full binary content of a specific document.

response.name: A list of document summaries for the patient.

variable.description: Patient ID (e.g. noah-wyle, tracy-ifeachor, patrick-ball, katherine-lanasa, supriya-ganesh, fiona-dourif, taylor-dearden, isa-briones, gerran-howell, shabana-azeez, sepideh-moafi, ayesha-harris, shawn-hatosy, jalen-thomas-brooks, kristin-villanueva, brandon-mendez-homer). request.description: Returns a unified chronological timeline of all resource types (conditions, medications, observations, procedures, documents) for the given patient, sorted newest-first. Each entry includes the resource type, date, and the full serialized resource payload.

response.name: A unified timeline of events for the patient.

variable.description: Patient ID (e.g. noah-wyle, tracy-ifeachor, patrick-ball, katherine-lanasa, supriya-ganesh, fiona-dourif, taylor-dearden, isa-briones, gerran-howell, shabana-azeez, sepideh-moafi, ayesha-harris, shawn-hatosy, jalen-thomas-brooks, kristin-villanueva, brandon-mendez-homer). request.description: Returns full patient details for the given patient ID. Patient IDs are case-insensitive.

response.name: Full patient details.

request.description: Returns a list of all patient summaries in the system.

response.name: A list of patient summaries.

variable.description: The binary resource ID.

request.description: Returns the raw binary content for the given binary resource ID. The response Content-Type header reflects the binary's original MIME type.

response.name: Raw binary content.

variable.description: The binary resource ID.

response.name: Binary found but could not be loaded or decoded.

variable.description: The binary resource ID.

response.name: The requested resource was not found.

variable.description: The binary resource ID.

request.description: Returns a list of all binary resource metadata entries in the store. Binary resources back patient documents and can be fetched by ID via GET /binaries/{binary_id}.

response.name: A list of binary resource metadata entries.

request.description: Returns a report of data quality issues detected in the FHIR data, including parse errors, missing required fields, unresolvable references, indeterminate statuses, case mismatch references, duplicate resources, amended resources, invalid fields, and non-standard resource types.

response.name: A list of data quality issues found in the FHIR data.
